_comment: EXAMPLE JVM configuration for the JVM-worker tutorial.
_how_to_use: To run Java calculators: (1) pip install py4j, (2) build the JAR with java/build.sh, (3) back up config/jvm_config.json and copy this file over it, (4) restart the server. The shipped config/jvm_config.json has the JVM manager DISABLED by default.

jvm_manager._enabled_note: true = manage JVM subprocesses and Py4J gateways automatically.
jvm_manager._auto_start_note: Start the JVM(s) when the server boots.
jvm_manager._config_check_note: Re-read this file every 10 minutes; restart affected JVMs if it changed.

gateways.name: primary
gateways._pool_size_note: THIS IS THE JVM WORKER POOL: 4 gateway server instances on ports 25333-25336, each a concurrent lane into the JVM. More lanes = more Java calculator calls in flight at once.

jvm._java_home_note: Path to a JDK. null = use the system default 'java' on PATH.
jvm._classpath_note: Run java/build.sh first to produce java/lib/dishtayantra-calculators.jar (and download py4j.jar).

gateways.name: secondary
gateways._enabled_note: Enable a SECOND JVM pool for isolation or extra throughput (e.g. keep heavy risk jobs off the primary JVM). Java calculators choose a pool via gateway_config.gateway.
